STIPULATION TO CONT. TR. FILED BY IVINS, LIZA ENOVITY, INC. TOOLWORKS, INC.
OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S MTN CONTESTING GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT ENOVITY, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS COMPLAINANT TOOLWORKS, INC. MOTION CONTESTING CROSS-DEFENDANTS APPLICATION FOR GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION CONTESTING THE GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS DENIED. SETTLOR HAS PROVIDED SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT A CRITICAL ASSUMPTION AS TO THE NATURE AND EXTENT OF ITS LIABILITY. PLAINTIFF FELL BECAUSE SHE TRIPPED ON A MAT, AND AFTER SHE FELL, THE DOOR CONTINUED TO PUSH HER. THERE IS NO SHOWING, HOWEVER, THAT THE DOOR CONTRIBUTED TO HER INJURIES, THAT THE DOOR MALFUNCTIONED, THAT ENOVITY WAS RESPONSIBLE FOR ANY SORT OF MALFUNCTION, OR THAT ENOVITY IN ITS MAINTENANCE OF THE DOOR OR OTHERWISE, WAS NEGLIGENT. ENOVITY HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT THE SETTLEMENT WAS IN THE BALLPARK AND TOOLWORKS FAILS TO MEET ITS BURDEN UNDER CCP SECTION 877.6(D). (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUN-24-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-04-2010 Continued To Oct-18-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-04-2010 Continued To Sep-24-10 At 01:00 P.M. In Dept 611

Notice And Motion Contesting X-Deft'S Application For Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Jun-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS COMPLAINANT TOOLWORKS, INC. Motion Contesting Cross-Defendants Application for Good Faith Settlement. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion contesting the good faith settlement is denied. Settlor has provided substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of its liability. Plaintiff fell because she tripped on a mat, and after she fell, the door continued to push her. There is no showing, however, that the door contributed to her injuries, that the door malfunctioned, that Enovity was responsible for any sort of malfunction, or that Enovity in its maintenance of the door or otherwise, was negligent. Enovity has presented evidence showing that the settlement was in the ballpark and Toolworks fails to meet its burden under CCP section 877.6(d). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to Defendant's Counsel Elizabeth A. Skane for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is ordered off calendar and dissolved/Case removed from the Early Settlement Conference. Proceedings not reported. (212)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-10-2009 Set For Trial On Aug-02-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Jul-16-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 611
